summary: Implemented a major Classic mode decomp slice in `gmclassic.c` with supporting type/header cleanup. New C covers matchup order/random selection, Classic OnLoad setup, intro/preload setup, match launch, and match exit/record update logic. decomp.dev reported 3 new 100% matches (`gmClassic_801B3B40`, `gmClassic_801B3A34`, `gmclassic` `.sdata`) and 4 partial improvements (`gmClassic_801B3500` 92.27%, `gmClassic_OnLoad` 84.00%, `gmClassic_801B2D54` 99.96%, `gmClassic_801B2BA4` 93.87%), for +784 matched code bytes and +24 matched data bytes.

key_files.role: Main Classic mode decomp work; adds typed local data structs and C implementations for matchup setup, OnLoad, intro/preload, match start, and match exit handling.
Shared type cleanup; typed `UnkAdventureData`/`UnkAllstarData` callbacks and replaced `gm_803DDEC8Struct` bitfield/blob layout with explicit fields plus a `gmClassicMatchup` pointer.
Header cleanup; removed internal/static helper declarations and the bare `gmClassic_803DDEC8` global declaration.
Prototype formatting for `gm_8017DB88` and `fn_8017DD7C`, including callback argument types used by Classic setup.
Adjusted `UnkAllstarData.x58` assignment from integer `0` to `NULL` after the field became a function pointer.

what_changed: Replaced placeholder comments for `gmClassic_801B2BA4`, `gmClassic_801B2D54`, `gmClassic_OnLoad`, `gmClassic_801B3500`, `gmClassic_801B3A34`, and `gmClassic_801B3B40` with C in `src/melee/gm/gmclassic.c`.

Added file-local Classic data models: `gmClassicMatchup`, `gmClassicMatchupData`, `gmClassicIntroData`, `gmClassic_80490880Data`, and `gmClassic_803DDEC8Data`, all with offset comments and `STATIC_ASSERT` size checks.
Added `gmClassic_InitMatchupOrder`, which counts matchup arrays until sentinel stage id `0x148`, fills an order array, and shuffles it with `HSD_Randi`.

Implemented `gmClassic_801B2BA4` matchup selection over shuffled pools, filtering empty character slots `0x21`, the current player character, `gm_80164430`, `gm_80164840`, already-used matchup characters, and stage comparisons through `Stage_8022519C`.

Implemented `gmClassic_801B2D54` to assign `gm_803DDEC8Struct.xC` matchups according to flag bits, including fixed entries `gm_804D4318`, `gm_804D4320`, `gm_804D4328`, and the `x0C0` pool for `x1 & 0x20`.

Implemented `gmClassic_OnLoad` to clear matchup pointers, initialize matchup order arrays `x80/x74/x54/x2C`, zero `gm_804908A0`, set `UnkAllstarData` callback fields `x48..x70`, set minor scene `0x70`, and call `Ground_801C5A28`.

Implemented `gmClassic_801B3500` to populate Classic intro/preload data: enemy and ally arrays, enemy color setup via `gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable`, `lbDvd` game cache entries, special stage selection, and `lbAudioAx` audio preload masks.

Implemented `gmClassic_801B3A34` to prepare `StartMeleeData` through `gm_8017CE34` and `gm_8016F088`; implemented `gmClassic_801B3B40` to write per-round status to `gm_804908A0`, check final clear via `gm_8017D7AC`, and update special-case timing/best-record fields.

Changed `types.h` so `UnkAdventureData` and `UnkAllstarData` callback slots are concrete function-pointer types instead of `UNK_T`/`u32`, and changed `gm_803DDEC8Struct` from bitfields/blob padding to explicit byte/u16 fields plus `struct gmClassicMatchup* xC`.
Changed `gmClassic_801B3E44` to use `gmClassic_803DDEC8.x00` after the global was modeled as a typed aggregate rather than a raw `gm_803DDEC8Struct`.
smart_moves: Modeled data layout first with exact-size local structs and `STATIC_ASSERT`, making the later pointer arithmetic and array indexing evidence-backed instead of raw `UNK_T` access.
Forward-declared `struct gmClassicMatchup` in `types.h` while keeping the actual `gmClassicMatchup` layout private to `gmclassic.c`, avoiding unnecessary header exposure.
Made `gmClassic_801B2BA4` and `gmClassic_801B2D54` `static` and removed their public header declarations, keeping `gmclassic.h` focused on externally used scene callbacks.
Typed callback fields in `UnkAllstarData`/`UnkAdventureData` before implementing calls through them; this also exposed the need to use `NULL` instead of integer `0` in `gmallstar.c`.

Preserved low-level control-flow shape where useful for matching: sentinel loops, `goto next` in matchup selection, explicit casts, empty infinite loops on impossible no-match paths, and `PAD_STACK` in stack-sensitive functions.

Kept preload side effects in the same setup routine as the original behavior appears to do: game cache population, stage id selection, and audio bitmask construction are all handled inside `gmClassic_801B3500`.
decomp_lessons: For `gm` mode data blobs, model the aggregate layout with offset comments and size asserts before decomping behavior; `gmClassic_803DDEC8` turned out to contain several adjacent arrays/pools, not just one simple table.

When code mostly tests flag masks such as `x1 & 8`, `x1 & 0x20`, or `x1 == 0x80`, a plain `u8` flag field can be more matching-friendly than C bitfields; name individual bits later when semantics are confirmed.
Shared mode-state callback slots should be typed project-wide. Once `UnkAllstarData.x58` became a function pointer, other users such as All-Star needed pointer-correct initialization with `NULL`.
Classic matchup logic is sentinel-heavy: `0x148` terminates matchup lists, `0x21` marks empty character slots, and `0x0D` terminates `gm_803DDEC8Struct` sequences.

Large scene setup decomp may need exact casts to callback signatures, preload cache calls, and audio bitmask construction to get close to matching; `gmClassic_801B3500` reached 92.27% but still has remaining work.
The bot report is useful triage: `gmClassic_801B2D54` at 99.96% is a near-finish target, while `gmClassic_OnLoad` and `gmClassic_801B3500` likely need further type/control-flow refinement.
naming_conventions: New Classic-private structs use the `gmClassic` prefix: `gmClassicMatchup`, `gmClassicMatchupData`, `gmClassicIntroData`, `gmClassic_80490880Data`, and `gmClassic_803DDEC8Data`.
Unknown fields retain project-style offset names such as `x00`, `x02`, `x0C0`, `x1B8`, and `x2B0` until semantics are stronger.
Padding is named by offset, e.g. `pad_06`, `pad_1B6`, `pad_2AE`, and `pad_2EC`, matching the decomp convention used elsewhere.
Shared `types.h` keeps `gm_803DDEC8Struct` as the existing symbol name but upgrades it with offset comments and explicit field widths.
Public scene functions remain declared in `gmclassic.h` with address comments; file-internal helpers are `static`/`static inline` and are not exported.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in `gmClassic_801B3A34` and `PAD_STACK(4)` plus an unused local array in `gmClassic_801B3B40` to shape stack layout.
Used explicit casts to `(u8)`, `(u16)`, `(s8)`, `(InternalStageId)`, and callback function-pointer signatures to control codegen around comparisons and calls.
Kept a `goto next` structure in `gmClassic_801B2BA4`, matching the nested filter-and-skip flow visible in the decompiled C.
Used sentinel loops (`x00 != 0x148`, `x0 != 0x0D`) instead of hard-coded loop counts where the data layout indicates terminators.
Used scoped temporary variables such as `echar`, `achar`, `ap`, `stage_id`, and `new_var` in large functions, a common register/lifetime matching tactic.
Represented no-result error paths in `gmClassic_801B2D54` as empty infinite loops, preserving the apparent original control flow.
Changed raw aggregate access from `&gmClassic_803DDEC8` to `gmClassic_803DDEC8.x00` once the global was typed, avoiding pointer-type mismatches.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals were present in the provided slice; the PR body was empty.
decomp.dev reported matched code at 64.96% with +0.02% / +784 bytes for GALE01.
decomp.dev reported matched data at 38.55% with +24 bytes, including `main/melee/gm/gmclassic` `.sdata` at 100%.
Bot-listed new 100% matches: `gmClassic_801B3B40` (+516 bytes) and `gmClassic_801B3A34` (+268 bytes).

Bot-listed partial improvements: `gmClassic_801B3500` +1229 bytes to 92.27%, `gmClassic_OnLoad` +1128 bytes to 84.00%, `gmClassic_801B2D54` +547 bytes to 99.96%, and `gmClassic_801B2BA4` +405 bytes to 93.87%.
follow_up_queries: Why is `gmClassic_801B2D54` still at 99.96% rather than 100%, and is the remaining issue a compare/cast/control-flow detail?
What remaining type or control-flow refinements are needed for `gmClassic_OnLoad` to improve beyond 84.00%?
Can `gmClassic_801B3500` be finished by further typing `gmClassicIntroData`, `GameCache`, `PreloadCacheSceneEntry`, or callback signatures?
Should magic constants `0x148`, `0x21`, `0x0D`, `0xAF`, and flag masks `0x80/0x20/0x10/0x08/0x04/0x02` become named constants once semantics are confirmed?
Should `gmClassicMatchup` remain file-local, or do later Classic/All-Star/adventure decomp slices need the layout in a shared header?
Can `gm_803DDEC8Struct.x1` gain named bit masks without hurting matching once more users are decompiled?
